best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-comment-001 title: adding and retrieving comments module: comment source pages: 1,3,4 preconditions: post exists in the system. test data: - comment content: "great post!" - user id: 1 - post id: 1 test steps: 1. create a new comment with the given content and user. 2. associate the comment with the post. 3. store the comment in the database. 4. retrieve the comment from the database. 5. verify the comment content and user association. expected result: 1. comment should be created successfully. 2. comment should be associated with the post. 3. comment should be stored in the database. 4. retrieved comment should match the original content and user association.

test case id: tc-post-001 title: creating and viewing posts module: post source pages: 1,3,4 preconditions: user exists in the system. test data: - post content: "hello, world!" - user id: 1 - neighborhood id: 1 test steps: 1. create a new post with the given content and user. 2. store the post in the database. 3. retrieve the post from the database. 4. verify the post content and user association. expected result: 1. post should be created successfully. 2. post should be stored in the database. 3. retrieved post should match the original content and user association.

test case id: tc-chat-001 title: creating and managing chats module: chat source pages: 1,3,4 preconditions: users exist in the system. test data: - user id: 1 - chattedwith user id: 2 test steps: 1. create a new chatlist for the given users. 2. add the chattedwith user to the chatlist. 3. store the chatlist in the database. 4. retrieve the chatlist from the database. 5. verify the chatlist contains both users. expected result: 1. chatlist should be created successfully. 2. chattedwith user should be added to the chatlist. 3. chatlist should be stored in the database. 4. retrieved chatlist should contain both users.

test case id: tc-network-001 title: associating posts with neighborhoods module: network source pages: 1,3,4 preconditions: post and neighborhood exist in the system. test data: - post id: 1 - neighborhood id: 1 test steps: 1. associate the post with the neighborhood. 2. store the association in the database. 3. retrieve the association from the database. 4. verify the association between post and neighborhood. expected result: 1. association should be created successfully. 2. association should be stored in the database. 3. retrieved association should match the original post and neighborhood.

test case id: tc-user-001 title: user creation and authentication module: user source pages: 1,2 preconditions: test data: - user details: id=1, nome="john", cognome="doe", email="john.doe@example.com", photo="http://example.com/photo.jpg" - valid credentials: username="john.doe", password="password123" - invalid credentials: username="invalid.user", password="wrongpassword" test steps: 1. call usercreateprocedure with valid user details. 2. call storeddatadb with "valid credentials" for authorization. 3. call usercreateprocedure with invalid user details. 4. call storeddatadb with "not exist" for authentication. expected result: 1. usercreateprocedure should create a new user entry in storeddatadb. 2. storeddatadb should return authorization success. 3. usercreateprocedure should handle invalid user details gracefully. 4. storeddatadb should return authentication success.
